---
title: "4.16 Recursion"
subtitle: "Part 2: Factorial Method"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Algorithm Idea

### Factorial is defined recursively

For positive integers:

```text
factorial(1) = 1
factorial(n) = n * factorial(n - 1)
```

This definition directly maps to a recursive method.

## Factorial Source Code

### Recursive factorial

```java
public static int factorial(int n)
{
    if (n == 1)
    {
        return 1;
    }
    else
    {
        return n * factorial(n - 1);
    }
}
```

Source precondition used for tracing: `n >= 1`.

## Focused Trace

### Expand `factorial(4)`

```text
factorial(4)
4 * factorial(3)
4 * 3 * factorial(2)
4 * 3 * 2 * factorial(1)
4 * 3 * 2 * 1
24
```

The calls expand until the base case, then returned values are multiplied on the way back.

## Base Case Mistakes

### The condition must match the shrinking argument

Bug:

```java
public static int factorial(int n)
{
    return n * factorial(n - 1);
}
```

There is no base case, so the calls do not stop.

## Quick Check

### Trace returned values

What does `factorial(3)` return?

```text
factorial(3)
3 * factorial(2)
3 * 2 * factorial(1)
3 * 2 * 1
```

Answer: `6`.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- write a recursive factorial method with a base case
- identify the recursive case
- trace both call expansion and return values
- explain why `n - 1` moves toward the base case
- state the input assumption or handle invalid input
:::

## End

### Continue or return to the course outline

[Next: Tracing Recursion](4-16-part-3-tracing-recursion.html)  
[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
